100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 10, 1926, the British labor movement faced a significant strike as thousands of coal miners halted work, demanding better wages and working conditions. In the United States, the burgeoning aviation industry celebrated as Charles Lindbergh prepared for his historic transatlantic flight, capturing the public's imagination. Meanwhile, political tensions rose in Germany as the government grappled with economic instability and the aftermath of World War I.
